name: Tortle
summary: Tortles have a saying: “We wear our homes on our backs.” These turtle folk live on many worlds, most often journeying up and down coasts, along waterways, and across the sea. Tortles don’t have a unified story of how they were created, but they all have a sense of being mystically connected to the natural world. Carrying their shelter on their backs gives tortles a special feeling of security wherever they go, for even if they visit a far, unknown country, they have a place to lay their heads.

stats.label: Ability Score Increase
stats.value: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

stats.label: Size
stats.value: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

stats.label: Languages
stats.value: Your character can speak, read, and write Common and one other language that you and your DM agree is appropriate for the character. ThePlayer’s Handbookoffers a list of languages to choose from. The DM is free to modify that list for a campaign.

stats.label: Ability Score Increase
stats.value: Your Strength score increases by 2, and your Wisdom score increases by 1.

stats.label: Size
stats.value: Tortle adults stand 5 to 6 feet tall and average 450 pounds. Their shells account for roughly one-third of their weight. Your size is Medium.

coreBonuses: Ability Score Increase.When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.
Size.You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

Languages.Your character can speak, read, and write Common and one other language that you and your DM agree is appropriate for the character. ThePlayer’s Handbookoffers a list of languages to choose from. The DM is free to modify that list for a campaign.
Ability Score Increase.Your Strength score increases by 2, and your Wisdom score increases by 1.
Size.Tortle adults stand 5 to 6 feet tall and average 450 pounds. Their shells account for roughly one-third of their weight. Your size is Medium.
Speed.Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
Languages.You can speak, read, and write Common and Aquan.

coreTraits.name: Claws
coreTraits.summary: You have claws that you can use to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier slashing damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

coreTraits.name: Hold Breath
coreTraits.summary: You can hold your breath for up to 1 hour.

coreTraits.name: Natural Armor
coreTraits.summary: Your shell provides you a base AC of 17 (your Dexterity modifier doesn’t affect this number). You can’t wear light, medium, or heavy armor, but if you are using a shield, you can apply the shield’s bonus as normal.

coreTraits.name: Nature's Intuition
coreTraits.summary: Thanks to your mystical connection to nature, you gain proficiency with one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Stealth, or Survival.

coreTraits.name: Shell Defense
coreTraits.summary: You can withdraw into your shell as an action. Until you emerge, you gain a +4 bonus to your AC, and you have advantage on Strength and Constitution saving throws. While in your shell, you are prone, your speed is 0 and can’t increase, you have disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws, you can’t take reactions, and the only action you can take is a bonus action to emerge from your shell.

coreTraits.name: Age
coreTraits.summary: Young tortles crawl for a few weeks after birth before learning to walk on two legs. They reach adulthood by the age of 15 and live an average of 50 years.

coreTraits.name: Alignment
coreTraits.summary: Tortles tend to lead orderly, ritualistic lives. They develop customs and routines, becoming more set in their ways as they age. Most are lawful good. A few can be selfish and greedy, tending more toward evil, but it's unusual for a tortle to shuck off order in favor of chaos.

coreTraits.name: Claws
coreTraits.summary: Your claws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

coreTraits.name: Hold Breath
coreTraits.summary: You can hold your breath for up to 1 hour at a time. Tortles aren't natural swimmers, but they can remain underwater for some time before needing to come up for air.

coreTraits.name: Natural Armor
coreTraits.summary: Due to your shell and the shape of your body, you are ill-suited to wearing armor. Your shell provides ample protection, however; it gives you a base AC of 17 (your Dexterity modifier doesn't affect this number). You gain no benefit from wearing armor, but if you are using a shield, you can apply the shield's bonus as normal.

notes: raw text is preserved in page.txt for manual inspection
subrace extraction is heuristic; inspect outputs for edge cases
